daily.wisdom: The most radical act is to be entirely, unapologetically yourself in a world that profits from your self-doubt.
daily.perspective: Growth is not construction. It is excavation. You are not building something new — you are uncovering what was always there, set down for safekeeping.
daily.journal: I've had this growing realization that a lot of my life was spent dimming myself down. Not because I wanted to be invisible, but because I learned that being fully visible came with consequences. What's been happening recently feels like a thaw — a warming up of something that had been frozen in place for a long time. I'm not trying to build a new identity. I'm remembering one I set down for safekeeping.
daily.poetry:
I learned to edit before I spoke
to think twice before I felt
to fold myself into rooms
that were not built for my wingspan.

But compression is not peace.
And now the thaw —
not explosive, not dramatic,
just: a door left open
that I forgot I had the key to.

music.title: iatric_MX — Session 001
music.description: First daily mix. Raw, unfiltered, made while the world was quiet.

daily.free_offering: Today I'm offering: one free voiceover recording for anyone who needs a voice for their project — documentary, audiobook, meditation, short film. Just ask.
daily.life_improvement: Close one tab you've had open for more than a week that you haven't actually read. You're not going to. And that's fine. The relief is the point.

artist.bio_short: Mikel Rosenthal is a polymath and Fluxus artist working across music, performance, visual art, design, and voice. He makes music while he works, records it by accident, and calls that a method. He is ambidextrous, hyperfantasiac, and can replicate any sound he hears — human, animal, mechanical — usually on the first try.
artist.bio_long:
PitchBlacKnight is the singular creative identity of Mikel Rosenthal — Senior UI/UX Designer, music producer (iatric_MX), voice performer, Fluxus artist, and estate sale archivist. His practice collapses the boundaries between disciplines the way Fluxus always intended: not as a manifesto, but as a way of breathing.

He has been compared to Yoko Ono — not because he seeks comparison, but because he cannot help making everything into art. He collects discarded personal documents from estate sales of the recently deceased, transforming them into multi-panel fine art series that bear the names of the people they belonged to. He gives back what the world was about to throw away.

He has hyper-fantasia: an inner world so vivid and texturally rich it operates as a parallel reality. He is a phonetic mimic who can absorb and reproduce accents, dialects, animal sounds, and mechanical textures after a single exposure. He picks up languages with unnerving speed — not just words but the music of how they're spoken.

He makes music because silence while working feels like a missed opportunity. He makes art from other people's leftovers because everything deserves a second life. He offers things for free because he can, and because generosity is its own form of performance art.

shop.note: Shop coming soon — digital prints, T-shirts, and estate sale fine art series.
